newsSinceJun6: Capital Markets Day held June 8 in Örebro: Epiroc disclosed 3,900 driverless machines now in the field, 40 sites running battery-electric vehicles, 43% of offering available fossil-free (full offering by 2030), and reiterated automation/digitalization/electrification as core strategy. Alongside CMD, Ericsson and Epiroc expanded their partnership into a broader go-to-market alliance, with Epiroc selling Ericsson LTE/5G private-network connectivity as part of its digital portfolio for surface and underground mines. Stock fell -3.8% on June 9 — looks like post-CMD 'sell the news' digestion (consensus analyst target ~SEK 210 sits below the price; no new financial-target upgrade emerged), not a thesis break.

verdicts.rankRationale: For a latent-AI-node screen, the Jun 8 CMD delivered genuinely new evidence on exactly this thesis — 3,900 driverless machines deployed, Ericsson 5G alliance expanded into a go-to-market deal, LinkOA autonomy extending into quarrying — while the -3.8% post-CMD sell-the-news dip plus a YTD pullback from +28.4% to +23.6% improves entry asymmetry on an intact thesis.
verdicts.bull: Epiroc is quietly becoming the autonomy/connectivity OEM for mines: a 3,900-unit driverless fleet, an Ericsson private-5G distribution alliance, and LinkOA pushing beyond mining into quarrying give it real optionality on AI-driven metals demand without being priced as an AI name.
verdicts.neutral: The AI linkage remains indirect — mining automation traction is real but the demand driver is broad metals capex, not AI compute specifically; consensus targets below the share price suggest the market sees fair value here.
verdicts.bear: A mining-capex downcycle or copper price rollover hits orders regardless of automation share; CMD brought no financial-target upgrade and analysts' ~SEK 210 consensus implies downside, so the rerating may already be done.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 order intake confirming automation/digital momentum post-CMD, or a large AHS/LinkOA fleet win tied to copper expansion projects.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mining capex cycle softening (copper/gold price retreat) compressing equipment orders and exposing the post-CMD valuation with consensus targets already below the price.
